codeReview.summary: Implementation meets the feature intent: resetState now performs broad cleanup (active PDF destroy, blob URL revocation, canvas release, state clearing), DocumentViewer releases previous preview canvas before rendering the next page, and download blob URLs are tracked for reset-time revocation. The redaction pipeline remains sequential and releases page/canvas resources per page.

issues.description: The new 'sequential redaction pipeline' verification is mostly shape-based (export/import checks) and does not directly assert the render->burn->embed->release->cleanup ordering in redactDocument, so regressions in true sequential behavior may not be caught by this test file.

sharedStateObservations.observation: ui-worker requires test-first (RED -> GREEN), but this run implemented core code edits before creating the new cleanup test file; handoff skillFeedback still reports followedProcedure=true.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: worker-transcripts.jsonl:33 shows multiple Edit calls in src/app/state.ts, src/components/DocumentViewer.tsx, and src/components/FooterBar.tsx before the later 'Now let me write the unit tests' step; handoff skillFeedback marks followedProcedure true.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Agent-browser CLI usage details are still easy to get wrong and caused trial-and-error during verification; shared guidance could include concrete command forms.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: worker-transcripts.jsonl:33 includes failed commands 'agent-browser ... --url' (unknown command) and 'screenshot --path ...' (timeout/usage correction) before successful 'open' and positional 'screenshot <path>' usage.

summary: Reviewed cleanup-and-memory (commit 931e038): behavior is functionally aligned with the feature requirements and no blocking defects were found. One non-blocking test-depth gap was identified, plus two shared-state observations around skill adherence reporting and agent-browser command guidance.
